description:
Second half of F2 (performance), now under epic mutagen-xez (split out of mutagen-33b). Consume the F2a index to run, per mutant, only the test modules whose covered line-set intersects the mutated line -- instead of re-running the full cited suite for every one of S sites.

Trigger: any run citing a suite larger than a few tests. Blast radius today: S*N test executions where O(S*k) is achievable; on 200 sites x 500 tests that is ~100k vs a few thousand.

Files: lib/mix/tasks/mutagen.ex:947 (mutation input -- thread the index in), lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner.ex:736, lib/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner/mutation_loop.ex:194-214 (today registers ALL test_modules + runs ex_unit.run(); register/run only covering modules instead).

## Approach
Per site: covering = index[site.file][site.line] (MapSet of test modules). site_test_modules = filter cfg.test_modules to covering. Register/run only those. On EMPTY/missing index for a line -> FALL BACK to all cited modules (safety: never under-test a mutant).

## Correctness -- the load-bearing risk (review findings H3 + M3)
Coverage-driven per-mutant selection is the industry-standard technique (PIT/Stryker/mutmut) and is sound for WELL-ISOLATED suites with accurate line coverage. But the equivalence is NOT free, and two residual failure modes must be gated, not assumed:
- NON-empty-but-wrong: index[file][line] is non-empty but omits the module whose assertion actually kills the mutant (e.g. a test module covers the line for setup but a DIFFERENT module kills via cross-test shared state). The empty-index fallback does NOT fire here -- it only guarantees "never run zero tests", not "never under-test". This is a false survivor: the worst mutation-testing error.
- Coverage line-attribution imprecision at module granularity.

## Parity gate (replaces "one fixture" -- a single implementer-authored fixture proves only the easy case)
Done REQUIRES BOTH:
(a) A PROJECT-WIDE DIFFERENTIAL gate: run narrowed AND full suite across the real corpus, assert per-mutant kill/survive EQUALITY on EVERY mutant (not one fixture).
(b) An ADVERSARIAL fixture where a covering test does NOT assert and a non-covering-by-line test DOES kill -- demonstrating the gate CAN fail. If the team cannot construct (b) and show the design handles it, the equivalence claim is unproven.

## Done
- Each mutant runs only its covering test modules, with empty-index -> all-tests fallback.
- (a) project-wide differential parity gate green: narrowed == full-suite kill/survive on every mutant.
- (b) adversarial false-survivor fixture exists and the gate detects it (proving the gate is not vacuous).
- (Optional) telemetry/log of tests-run-per-site so the perf win is observable.
title: F2b: Narrow test_modules per site from the coverage index (O(S*N) -> O(S*k))
